2604.26460.title: Theory-Grounded Evaluation Exposes the Authorship Gap in LLM Personalization
2604.26460.paper_summary: Stylistic personalization - making LLMs write in a specific individual's style, rather than merely adapting to task preferences - lacks evaluation grounded in authorship science. We show that grounding evaluation in authorship verification theory transforms what benchmarks can measure. Drawing on three measurement traditions - LUAR, a trained authorship verification model; an LLM-as-judge with decoupled trait matching; and classical function-word stylometrics - we evaluate four inference-time personalization methods across 50 authors and 1,000 generations. The theory-grounded metric, LUAR, provides what ad hoc alternatives cannot: calibrated baselines, with a human ceiling of 0.756 and a cross-author floor of 0.626, that give scores absolute meaning. All methods score below this floor, from 0.484 to 0.508, exposing an authorship gap invisible to uncalibrated metrics. The three metrics produce near-zero pairwise correlations, with absolute r less than 0.07, confirming that without theoretical grounding, metric choice determines conclusions: an LLM judge declares a clear winner while LUAR finds no meaningful differentiation. These findings demonstrate the theory-benchmark cycle in action: authorship theory exposes evaluation failures that ad hoc benchmarks miss.
2604.26460.paper_summary_zh: 風格個性化 - 使大型語言模型以特定個體的風格寫作，而不僅僅是適應任務偏好 - 缺乏基於作者身份科學的評估。我們展示了將評估基於作者身份驗證理論如何改變基準能夠測量的內容。基於三種測量傳統 - LUAR，一個訓練過的作者身份驗證模型；一個具有解耦特徵匹配的LLM作為評判者；以及經典的功能詞風格測量 - 我們對50位作者和1,000次生成進行了四種推理時個性化方法的評估。基於理論的指標LUAR提供了臨時替代品所無法提供的：經過校準的基準，具有0.756的人類上限和0.626的跨作者下限，賦予分數絕對的意義。所有方法的得分均低於此下限，範圍從0.484到0.508，揭示了一個對於未經校準的指標來說是不可見的作者身份差距。這三個指標產生了近乎零的成對相關性，絕對r小於0.07，確認了在沒有理論基礎的情況下，指標選擇決定了結論：一個LLM評判者宣告出一個明確的贏家，而LUAR則未發現有意義的區分。這些發現展示了理論-基準循環的運作：作者身份理論揭示了臨時基準所忽略的評估失敗。
2604.26460.author: Yash Ganpat Sawant et.al.
